meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a SaaS pricing module to overcome the no portfolio trap. A Beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch fast.
content.answer_block: Building a SaaS pricing module is the ultimate portfolio hack. It demonstrates mastery of user psychology, API integration, and subscription logic—three core pillars of professional software engineering. By implementing a dynamic pricing table, you prove to employers that you can build functional, revenue-generating features from scratch.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of tech careers. You have the skills, but you lack the proof. Most students stagnate here, paralyzed by the 'what to build' dilemma, while the industry demands tangible evidence of shipping real-world utility.

Today, we break that cycle. By building a robust, dynamic SaaS pricing engine, you move beyond 'to-do list' tutorials and enter the realm of product engineering. This isn't just code; it's a strategic asset that signals to recruiters that you understand the mechanics of business, not just syntax.

steps.title: Define Tiered Data Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust JSON schema for pricing plans to ensure your frontend is decoupled from the data, making it easy to scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing plan including name, monthly price, annual price, feature list, and a boolean for 'isMostPopular'. Generate a mock data array with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate pricing-data.ts with defined interfaces and mock data'

steps.title: Build the Pricing Toggle Component
steps.description: Implement a monthly/annual toggle logic. This is a critical UI pattern that shows attention to user experience and conversion flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React functional component for a pricing toggle switch that flips between 'Monthly' and 'Yearly' billing cycles and updates the displayed price state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create PricingToggle.tsx component with state management'

steps.title: Develop the Pricing Card UI
steps.description: Create high-converting cards that highlight the 'Pro' plan. Focus on clean layout and responsive design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive grid of pricing cards using Tailwind CSS. Highlight the middle card with a border-glow effect to signify the 'Most Popular' choice.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement PricingGrid.tsx using Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout Simulation
steps.description: Simulate the payment gateway interaction to show you understand how SaaS applications handle sensitive transactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a mock async function 'handleCheckout' that logs user selection, plan ID, and billing cycle to the console, simulating a Stripe API call.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add handleCheckout function to pricing buttons'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: Ship your project to a live URL and write a 'README' that explains the business logic behind your pricing choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md that explains the technical decisions made, specifically focusing on the state management of the pricing toggle and the scalability of the data schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate professional README.md for the project'

pro_tips: Always add a 'Contact Sales' button for Enterprise tiers; it signals you understand B2B enterprise sales cycles.
Use CSS variables for your pricing colors so users can easily theme their own instances of your component.
Implement a 'Savings' badge that dynamically calculates the discount percentage when the user toggles to yearly billing.
Never hardcode prices in the UI; always fetch them from a config file or a mock API to simulate real-world environments.
Add a 'loading' state to your checkout button to demonstrate an understanding of async UI feedback loops.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, add a 'Case Study' section to your portfolio site describing the challenges you faced while building the pricing logic and how you solved them.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready feature that proves you can build professional-grade SaaS tools. Don't let this code sit in a private repo—share it, refine it, and start building your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
